title: Ottonia muelleri
taxonomy: Ottonia muelleri is classified in the kingdom Plantae, family Amaryllidaceae, and order Asparagales. It belongs to the family Amaryllidaceae which includes plants such as the lily and daffodil. Further classification places it in the genus Ottonia and species O. muelleri. The family Amaryllidaceae is part of the larger monocot clade, which includes plants with one cotyledon in their seeds. The order Asparagales includes a wide range of flowering plants, including asparagus and orchids.

intro: Ottonia muelleri, also known as Ottonia, is a medicinal plant species belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family, native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various diseases. The bioactive constituents of Ottonia muelleri include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, including ottonin, ottonine, and other flavonoids. In traditional herbal medicine, Ottonia is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its extracts are used in various preparations, such as capsules, tablets, and tinctures, to support immune system function and reduce inflammation.
